name: Dustin Poirier
winProfile: Big signature wins, lighter LW volume

qualityWinLedger.context: Elite champion-level opponent moving up for interim lightweight title.
Former two-division champion name with activity/timing context.
Former UFC champion and elite lightweight contender.

High-level name, but injury/weird ending context.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Khabib and Oliveira did not end it because Poirier recovered with more elite wins/title fights. Islam is endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Poirier returned with McGregor wins and another title shot.
Poirier later returned to elite lightweight relevance.
Poirier later earned the Islam title fight.
Retirement fight after the Islam endpoint.

shortCase: Poirier is the elite lightweight résumé case: one interim belt, a giant win ledger, brutal schedule strength, and no undisputed title to push him into the higher GOAT tier.
bestArgument: Poirier wins comparisons when deep win quality, modern lightweight strength, and repeated elite relevance matter more than undisputed title reign length.
counterArgument: Poirier’s counterargument in debates is that he beat more great fighters than some actual champions and kept taking the hardest available lightweight fights.
whyRankedHere: Poirier ranks here because the UFC-only résumé is loaded: 22 UFC wins, an interim lightweight title, two UFC wins over Max Holloway, two over Conor McGregor, and major lightweight wins over Justin Gaethje, Eddie Alvarez, Michael Chandler, Anthony Pettis, Dan Hooker, Benoit Saint Denis, and Jim Miller.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because he never won the undisputed UFC title, lost three undisputed lightweight title fights, and the Gaethje rematch plus Islam finish stack enough late elite damage that the -10 loss cap is doing real work.
finalTakeaway: Poirier is the elite résumé-over-belt case: he has enough UFC wins and opponent quality to sit around the top 20, but the missing undisputed title and capped loss context keep him below the true champion-reign tier.
